data.narrative:
Zap Energy is a Series D nuclear energy company founded by Benj Conway, Brian A. Nelson, and Uri Shumlak, currently valued at $1.0B post-money following $330M+ in cumulative fundraising. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 nuclear energy peers—3 public and 8 private—including TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, Nano Nuclear Energy, and Oklo, which span valuations from $200M to $4.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a standard 1.5x step-up multiplier from the $1.0B Series D valuation, implying $1.5B at the next funding stage. Peer-median analysis examines 6 same-sector comparables with similar valuation tiers; the median across this set stands at $1.6B, with a range spanning $200M (Type One Energy, Series B) to $4.0B (TerraPower, late stage). Both methods produce closely aligned outputs, supporting convergence on fair value.

The triangulated analysis yields a range of $1.3B (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $1.5B. This suggests Zap's current $1.0B post-money valuation sits below the implied range midpoint by approximately $500M. Key uncertainties include timing to next inflection event, relative technology maturity versus the peer set, and the wide dispersion in comparable valuations ($200M–$4.0B), which reflects heterogeneity in development stage and commercial readiness within the nuclear energy sector.
